title: VDD parsing: extract_json_from_response uses byte slicing that panics on multibyte input
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

Dimension: Functional Correctness / Error Orchestration. Location: src/vdd/parsing.rs:19-72 (extract_json_from_response). Violation: L24 uses text[json_start..json_start + end] where json_start = start + 7 (raw byte offset past the json fence). If an adversary response contains multibyte UTF-8 (emoji, CJK, smart quotes) near the fence, the byte offset may fall inside a codepoint. str slicing on a non-char-boundary panics. Same for L35 text[actual_start..actual_start + end] and L68 text[start..=end]. The adversary is an arbitrary upstream LLM producing natural-language prose — emoji and unicode are expected. A single emoji before the JSON crashes parse_findings which crashes the entire VDD loop. Mandated Refactor: Replace all byte-index slicing with char-boundary-safe access via text.get(range) returning Option, then handle None as a parse failure. Add a proptest fuzzing extract_json_from_response with arbitrary UTF-8 strings. Add a unit test with a 4-byte UTF-8 codepoint immediately before the fence.
